questions.q: Bhikaji Cama is often remembered by which honorific title?
questions.explanation: Bhikaji Cama is widely honoured as the 'Mother of the Indian Revolution' for her pioneering work abroad.

questions.q: At which 1907 international gathering did Bhikaji Cama unfurl an early version of an Indian flag?
options: The Round Table Conference in London
The International Socialist Congress in Stuttgart
questions.explanation: She unfurled an early Indian flag at the International Socialist Congress in Stuttgart, Germany, in 1907.

questions.q: In which year did Bhikaji Cama unfurl the flag at Stuttgart?
questions.explanation: The famous flag-unfurling at the Stuttgart Socialist Congress took place in 1907.

questions.q: From which continent did Bhikaji Cama mainly campaign for Indian independence?
questions.explanation: She campaigned for India's freedom largely from Europe, especially Britain, France, and Germany.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama was a pioneer of the freedom movement in which respect?
options: As an Indian activist working abroad
As a sepoy in the British army
questions.explanation: She was a pioneer of the freedom movement conducted abroad, building networks of Indian revolutionaries in Europe.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama was born into which community?
questions.explanation: Bhikaji Cama was born into a prominent Parsi family in Bombay.

questions.q: What did Bhikaji Cama campaign tirelessly for?
questions.explanation: She devoted her life to campaigning for India's independence from British rule.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Bhikaji Cama born?
questions.explanation: She was born in Bombay (present-day Mumbai) in 1861.

questions.q: The flag Bhikaji Cama unfurled at Stuttgart is considered an early version of what?
questions.explanation: Her Stuttgart flag is regarded as one of the early versions of the Indian national flag.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama is celebrated mainly as a what in the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: She is celebrated as a revolutionary and activist who internationalized the cause of Indian freedom.

questions.q: In which year was Bhikaji Cama born?
questions.explanation: Bhikaji Cama was born on 24 September 1861 in Bombay.

questions.q: While nursing patients during which epidemic did Bhikaji Cama herself fall seriously ill in Bombay?
questions.explanation: She contracted illness while working among victims of the 1896-97 bubonic plague in Bombay, prompting her move to Europe.

questions.q: In London, Bhikaji Cama worked closely with which 'Grand Old Man of India'?
questions.explanation: She served for a time as private secretary to Dadabhai Naoroji and was influenced by him in London.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama was associated with India House, a hub of Indian revolutionaries, in which city?
questions.explanation: She was linked to India House in London, a centre for Indian nationalist students before she shifted to Paris.

questions.q: Which revolutionary nationalist helped found India House in London, within Bhikaji Cama's circle?
questions.explanation: Shyamji Krishna Varma founded India House in London, a centre of revolutionary activity that Cama was associated with.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama helped publish and distribute a revolutionary newspaper named what?
questions.explanation: She helped publish the revolutionary journal 'Bande Mataram' from Europe to spread the nationalist message.

questions.q: To which European city did Bhikaji Cama shift the base of her revolutionary activity around 1909?
questions.explanation: She moved her activities to Paris, where she continued to support and shelter Indian revolutionaries.

questions.q: During the First World War, the British pressured Bhikaji Cama's host country to intern or restrict her. Which country was that?
questions.explanation: Living in France during WWI, she was placed under restrictions and effectively interned due to British pressure.

questions.q: In which year did Bhikaji Cama die?
questions.explanation: Bhikaji Cama died on 13 August 1936 in Bombay, shortly after being allowed to return to India.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama was allowed to return to India only near the end of her life, in which year?
questions.explanation: She was finally permitted to return to India in 1935, about a year before her death in 1936.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama's surname came from her marriage into which prominent Parsi family?
questions.explanation: She married Rustom Cama and took the surname of the well-known Parsi Cama family of Bombay.

questions.q: Which young revolutionary, associated with London's India House, was part of Bhikaji Cama's circle and helped design the flag?
questions.explanation: V. D. Savarkar and other India House revolutionaries were associated with the flag's design and Cama's circle.

questions.q: What was Bhikaji Cama's maiden (birth) family name before marriage?
questions.explanation: She was born Bhikaji Sorab Patel, daughter of the wealthy Parsi merchant Sorabji Framji Patel.

questions.q: The flag Bhikaji Cama unfurled at Stuttgart in 1907 prominently displayed which words?
questions.explanation: The 1907 flag bore the words 'Vande Mataram' across its central band along with symbols of India's provinces.

questions.q: The Stuttgart flag of 1907 featured how many lotuses and stars said to represent the provinces of British India?
questions.explanation: The flag carried eight lotuses (or stars), commonly said to represent the eight provinces of British India.

questions.q: Reportedly while unfurling the flag at Stuttgart, Bhikaji Cama appealed for support for the freedom of India and equality of which group?
options: All human beings, regardless of race or religion
questions.explanation: Her Stuttgart appeal called for India's independence and human equality, addressing a socialist international audience.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama reportedly co-designed the 1907 flag with the help of which fellow revolutionary?
options: Shyamji Krishna Varma (and the India House circle)
questions.explanation: The flag is associated with Cama and the India House revolutionaries around Shyamji Krishna Varma and Savarkar.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama left a portion of her estate to support which institution in India?
options: A Parsi (Avabai Framji Petit / girls') charitable cause
questions.explanation: She left much of her wealth to Parsi charitable and girls' welfare causes, reflecting her philanthropic concerns.

questions.q: In which European city did Bhikaji Cama spend much of her later exile, hosting Indian revolutionaries at her residence?
questions.explanation: From around 1909 she lived in Paris, where her home became a meeting place and refuge for Indian revolutionaries.

questions.q: Bhikaji Cama's flag of 1907 is sometimes described as having which three horizontal colour bands (top to bottom)?
questions.explanation: The 1907 flag had three bands, commonly described as green (top), saffron or golden (middle), and red (bottom).
